texto: In many ways. . .the completeness of biography, the achievement of its professionalization, is an ironic fiction, since no life can ever be known completely, nor would we want to know every fact about an individual. Similarly, no life is ever lived according to aesthetic proportions. The "plot" of a biography is superficially based on the birth, life and death of the subject; "character," in the vision of the author. Both are as much creations of the biographer, as they are of a novelist. We content ourselves with "authorized fictions.
explicacao: Citação de Ira Bruce Nadel sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “In many ways. . .the completeness of biography, the achievement of its professionalization, is an…” articula sucesso, accuracy, biography em registro de sucesso, com relevância para contextos como luto e biography.
